description_long: This is a large, four-engine commercial jet airliner painted in a distinctive blue, white, and silver livery with 'UNITED STATES OF AMERICA' emblazoned along the fuselage. The nose is rounded and painted blue, with a cockpit featuring multiple windows, and the tail fin displays the American flag. The wings are swept back and mounted low on the fuselage, each supporting two large turbofan engines beneath. The aircraft has a conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers extending from either side. The landing gear is not visible in the images, but the undercarriage is streamlined for flight. The overall design and markings suggest this is a representation of the iconic Air Force One aircraft.
description: A large, blue-and-white four-engine jet airliner with 'UNITED STATES OF AMERICA' markings and a prominent tail fin displaying the American flag.
description_view_0: Front view showing the rounded blue nose, cockpit windows, and the full wingspan with four underwing engines visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the 'UNITED STATES OF AMERICA' text, the American flag on the tail, and the full profile of the fuselage and engines.
description_view_2: Rear top-down view displaying the swept-back wings, tail assembly, and the overall streamlined shape of the aircraft.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the other side of the fuselage, engines, and tail details.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, swept-back wings with slight dihedral and no visible winglets.
attribute_engine_type: Four underwing turbofan engines, two per wing.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Landing gear not visible; likely retractable tricycle configuration based on aircraft type.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed multi-seat cockpit with multiple windows.
